title: Spring Boot Cheatsheet - Hinglish
description: Spring Boot ko samjho asaan Hinglish mein — production-ready apps banao bina boilerplate ke

concepts.name: Spring Boot Kya Hai
concepts.explanation: Spring Boot ek framework hai jo Spring applications banana asaan banata hai. IoC (Inversion of Control) ka matlab hai ki Spring tumhare objects create karke manage karta hai — tum sirf batao kya chahiye, baaki Spring dekh lega. Socho jaise tum restaurant mein jaake sirf order dete ho, kitchen ka kaam tum nahi karte.
keyPoints: @SpringBootApplication = teen annotations ek mein
Auto-configuration automatically dependencies detect karke configure karta hai
Embedded server (Tomcat) built-in aata hai — alag server install nahi karna
spring-boot-starter-* dependencies group karke deta hai — ek daalo, sab aaye
Convention over Configuration — zyaadatar defaults sahi hote hain

concepts.name: Application Properties
concepts.explanation: application.properties ya application.yml mein apni app ki settings likho. Jaise phone ki settings — port, database, logging sab yahan set karo.
concepts.code:
# application.properties
server.port=8080
spring.application.name=my-app

# Database Connection
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb
spring.datasource.username=root
spring.datasource.password=password

# JPA Settings
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
spring.jpa.show-sql=true

# Logging
logging.level.root=INFO
logging.level.com.myapp=DEBUG

# Custom Properties
app.name=My Application
app.version=1.0
keyPoints: application.properties ya application.yml use karo
@Value("${app.name}") se custom property inject karo
server.port se port change karo
Profile-specific: application-dev.properties, application-prod.properties
spring.profiles.active=dev se active profile set karo

concepts.name: Dependency Injection
concepts.explanation: DI ka matlab hai ki Spring tumhare objects ke dependencies khud provide karke deta hai. Tum sirf declare karo ki kya chahiye, Spring dhoondh ke de dega. Jaise tum office mein 'mujhe printer chahiye' bolo aur IT department de de — tum khud printer setup nahi karte.
keyPoints: Constructor Injection sabse best practice hai — final fields, testable code
@Autowired single constructor ke liye optional hai
Field Injection (@Autowired field pe) avoid karo — testing mein mushkil hoti hai
@Service, @Repository, @Component, @Controller sab spring beans banate hain
Spring IoC Container beans create, manage aur inject karta hai

concepts.name: Stereotype Annotations
concepts.explanation: Spring mein @Component, @Service, @Repository, @Controller — ye annotations Spring ko batate hain ki 'is class ka object bana ke manage karo'. Har ek ka specific role hai, lekin ultimately sab @Component hi hain. Jaise school mein sab teachers hain, lekin koi math ka, koi science ka.
keyPoints: @Component = generic bean, @Service = business logic, @Repository = data access
@Controller = web MVC, @RestController = REST API (JSON return karta hai)
@Repository automatically database exceptions translate karta hai
@ComponentScan in sab ko detect karta hai — @SpringBootApplication mein already hai
Best practice: sahi annotation use karo — code padh ke role clear hona chahiye

concepts.name: Bean Scope & Lifecycle
concepts.explanation: Bean scope define karta hai ki Spring ek bean ka kitne objects banayega. Default mein sirf EK object banta hai poori application mein (Singleton). Lifecycle callbacks se bean banne aur hatne pe kuch kaam kar sakte ho.
keyPoints: Singleton (default): poori app mein ek hi instance
Prototype: har injection pe naya instance
Request/Session: web apps mein per-request/per-session
@PostConstruct: bean ready hone ke baad initialization
@PreDestroy: bean destroy hone se pehle cleanup

concepts.name: Dependency Injection
concepts.explanation: Spring ek object factory hai — tumhe objects banana nahi padta, Spring de deta hai. Constructor injection best practice hai — dependencies explicit rehti hain aur testing easy hoti hai!
keyPoints: Constructor injection best hai — dependencies explicit aur immutable
@Autowired single constructor pe optional hai (Spring 4.3+)
@Qualifier("beanName") — same type ki multiple beans mein se choose karo
Field injection (@Autowired on field) kaam karta hai but testing mushkil hoti hai

concepts.name: @Bean, @Configuration aur @Value
concepts.explanation: Manually beans define karo @Configuration class mein. Properties inject karo @Value se ya @ConfigurationProperties se. Complex third-party objects configure karne ke liye perfect!
keyPoints: @Configuration class mein @Bean methods se beans define karo
@Primary — multiple beans mein se default select hoti hai
@Value("${property.key}") — application.properties se value inject karo
@Value("${key:default}") — default value bhi de sakte ho
@ConfigurationProperties type-safe property binding ke liye

concepts.name: REST Controller Basics
concepts.explanation: REST API banane ke liye @RestController use karo. Ye HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) ko Java methods se map karta hai. Socho jaise waiter (controller) order leta hai aur kitchen (service) se khana mangta hai.
keyPoints: @RestController = @Controller + @ResponseBody (JSON automatically)
@GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping — HTTP methods
@PathVariable URL se value nikalta hai: /users/{id} → id = 5
@RequestBody JSON body ko Java object mein convert karta hai
@RequestParam query parameters ke liye: /users?name=raj → name = 'raj'

concepts.name: Request Validation
concepts.explanation: User ka input validate karo taaki galat data database mein na jaaye. Bean Validation annotations se bahut asaan hai.
keyPoints: @Valid trigger karta hai validation — controller method mein lagao
@NotBlank, @NotNull, @Size, @Email — common validators
@RestControllerAdvice se global error handling karo
DTO (Data Transfer Object) use karo — entity directly expose mat karo
Custom validator bhi bana sakte ho @Constraint se

concepts.name: Exception Handling
concepts.explanation: Errors ko gracefully handle karo taaki user ko samajh aaye ki kya galat hua. Global exception handler se ek jagah pe sab errors manage karo.
keyPoints: @RestControllerAdvice poori app ke errors handle karta hai
@ExceptionHandler specific exception type handle karta hai
Custom exceptions banao meaningful error messages ke liye
Error response mein status, message, timestamp daalo
Generic Exception handler last mein rakho — catch-all jaisa

concepts.name: REST Controller aur Request Mapping
concepts.explanation: REST API ka dil hai controller! @GetMapping, @PostMapping etc. se HTTP methods map karo. PathVariable URL se data lo, RequestParam query string se lo.
keyPoints: @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping — HTTP methods ke liye
@PathVariable — URL path se value lo ({id} syntax)
@RequestParam — query string se value lo (?key=value)
@RequestBody — JSON body ko Java object mein convert karo
ResponseEntity se status code aur headers control karo

concepts.name: CORS Configuration
concepts.explanation: Browser sirf same-origin requests allow karta hai by default. CORS configure karo taaki frontend (different port ya domain pe) backend se communicate kar sake.
keyPoints: @CrossOrigin — controller ya method level pe CORS allow karo
WebMvcConfigurer se global CORS configuration karo
allowedOrigins — kaunse domains se requests allow hain
allowCredentials(true) — cookies aur auth headers ke saath

concepts.name: Async aur Response Customization
concepts.explanation: ResponseEntity se custom status codes, headers return karo. response.setBody, headers — sab control karo. Production applications mein proper HTTP responses bahut zaroori hain.
keyPoints: ResponseEntity.ok() — 200 status
ResponseEntity.created(uri) — 201 Created
ResponseEntity.noContent() — 204 No Content
ResponseEntity.notFound() — 404 Not Found
.header() se custom headers add karo

concepts.name: JPA Entity
concepts.explanation: JPA Entity ek Java class hai jo database table se map hoti hai. Har object ek row hai. Annotations se batao kaun sa field kaun sa column hai. Spring Data JPA se database operations bahut asaan ho jaate hain.
keyPoints: @Entity class ko database table se map karta hai
@Id primary key define karta hai
@GeneratedValue auto-increment ke liye
@Column constraints define karta hai (nullable, unique, length)
@Table(name) se custom table naam de sakte ho

concepts.name: Spring Data Repository
concepts.explanation: Repository interface likhne se Spring automatically implementation banata hai! Tum sirf method naam likho — Spring query khud banayega. Jaadui lag raha hai lekin sach hai!
keyPoints: JpaRepository extend karo — CRUD + pagination + sorting free!
Method naming convention: findBy + FieldName + Condition
And, Or, Like, OrderBy, Between sab method naam mein likh sakte ho
@Query se custom JPQL ya native SQL likhho
save() insert aur update dono karta hai (id hai toh update, nahi toh insert)

concepts.name: Entity Relationships
concepts.explanation: Database tables ke beech relationships define karo — OneToMany, ManyToOne, ManyToMany. Jaise ek User ke kai Orders ho sakte hain.
keyPoints: @OneToMany: ek se bahut — mappedBy use karo (owner side ManyToOne hai)
@ManyToOne: bahut se ek — JoinColumn foreign key define karta hai
@ManyToMany: JoinTable se junction table banao
FetchType.LAZY default rakho — eagerly sab load mat karo!
CascadeType.ALL se parent save = children bhi save

concepts.name: Security Configuration
concepts.explanation: Spring Security tumhari application ko surakshit banata hai — kon login kar sakta hai, kon kya access kar sakta hai, sab yahan configure hota hai. Socho building ka security system — gate pe guard, har floor pe access card.
keyPoints: SecurityFilterChain se HTTP security configure karo
permitAll() = koi bhi access kare, authenticated() = login chahiye
hasRole() role-based access control ke liye
BCryptPasswordEncoder hamesha use karo — plain text password KABHI nahi!
CSRF REST APIs ke liye disable karo, web forms ke liye enable rakho

concepts.name: JWT Authentication
concepts.explanation: JWT (JSON Web Token) stateless authentication ke liye use hota hai. Login pe token milta hai, har request mein wo token bhejo. Server ko session maintain nahi karna padta. Jaise concert ka pass — ek baar lelo, baar baar dikhao.
keyPoints: JWT = Header + Payload + Signature (Base64 encoded)
Login pe token generate karo, har request mein Authorization header mein bhejo
Stateless — server pe session nahi rakhna padta
Token expiry set karo — kabhi na expire hone wala token security risk hai!
Secret key strong rakho aur environment variables mein store karo

concepts.name: Unit Testing
concepts.explanation: Ek ek class/method ko isolation mein test karo. Dependencies ko mock karo taaki sirf apna code test ho. Jaise ek car ka brake SIRF brake test karo — engine ke baare mein mat socho.
keyPoints: AAA pattern: Arrange (setup), Act (execute), Assert (verify)
@Mock fake dependency banata hai
@InjectMocks mocks inject karke test object banata hai
when().thenReturn() se mock ka behavior define karo
verify() se check karo ki method call hua ya nahi

concepts.name: Integration Testing
concepts.explanation: Poori app ko test karo — controller se lekar database tak. Real HTTP request bhejo aur response check karo. Ye ensure karta hai ki saare layers saath mein sahi kaam kar rahe hain.
keyPoints: @SpringBootTest poora Spring context load karta hai
MockMvc se HTTP requests simulate karo bina server start kiye
jsonPath() se JSON response verify karo
@AutoConfigureMockMvc MockMvc auto-configure karta hai
H2 in-memory database use karo testing ke liye

concepts.name: Database Setup
concepts.explanation: Spring Boot mein database connect karna bahut asaan hai — bas properties file mein URL, username, password daalo aur Spring baaki handle kar lega.
concepts.code:
# MySQL Configuration
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb
spring.datasource.username=root
spring.datasource.password=password
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

# JPA/Hibernate Settings
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update    # auto table create/update
spring.jpa.show-sql=true                # SQL queries console pe dikhao
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=true  # Formatted SQL

# H2 In-Memory Database (Testing ke liye)
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:h2:mem:testdb
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=org.h2.Driver
spring.h2.console.enabled=true          # H2 console browser mein kholo
keyPoints: ddl-auto=update tables auto-create/update karta hai
ddl-auto=validate production mein use karo — changes verify hote hain
H2 in-memory database testing ke liye perfect hai
show-sql=true development mein queries dekhne ke liye
Connection pooling (HikariCP) Spring Boot mein default hai

concepts.name: Transaction Management
concepts.explanation: @Transactional se database operations ko ek unit mein wrap karo. Agar kuch bhi galat ho, sab rollback ho jaayega. Jaise bank transfer — dono accounts update hone chahiye, ya koi nahi.
keyPoints: @Transactional method pe lagao — sab operations atomic honge
RuntimeException pe auto rollback hota hai
Checked exceptions pe rollback NAHI hota (unless specify karo)
readOnly=true read-only transactions ke liye performance improve karta hai
Service layer pe lagao, controller pe nahi — business logic mein

concepts.name: Spring Boot DevTools
concepts.explanation: DevTools se development tez hoti hai — code save karo aur app automatically restart ho jaati. Browser bhi auto-refresh hota hai. Production mein disabled rehta hai.
concepts.code:
<!-- pom.xml mein add karo -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
    <scope>runtime</scope>
    <optional>true</optional>
</dependency>

# application.properties
# LiveReload enable karo
spring.devtools.restart.enabled=true
spring.devtools.livereload.enabled=true

# Certain files pe restart skip karo
spring.devtools.restart.exclude=static/**,public/**
keyPoints: Auto-restart: code change pe app automatically restart hota hai
LiveReload: browser auto-refresh hota hai
H2 console auto-enable hota hai devtools ke saath
Production mein automatically disabled rehta hai
optional=true se dependent projects mein nahi jaata

concepts.name: Lombok
concepts.explanation: Lombok boilerplate code hata deta hai — getters, setters, constructors, toString sab annotations se generate ho jaate hain. Code chhota aur clean ho jaata hai.
keyPoints: @Data = @Getter + @Setter + @ToString + @EqualsAndHashCode
@Builder elegant object creation ke liye
@Slf4j automatic logger field add karta hai
@NoArgsConstructor, @AllArgsConstructor constructors generate karte hain
@RequiredArgsConstructor final fields ke liye constructor (DI ke liye perfect!)

concepts.name: Spring Boot Actuator
concepts.explanation: Actuator tumhari app ki health check karta hai — jaise doctor app ka checkup karta hai. Endpoints milte hain jo batate hain app zinda hai, kitni memory use ho rahi hai, env variables kya hain, etc.
concepts.code:
<!-- pom.xml mein add karo -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.properties
# Saare endpoints enable karo
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics,env

# Health details dikhao
management.endpoint.health.show-details=always

# Custom info
info.app.name=My Application
info.app.version=1.0.0
info.app.description=Spring Boot App
keyPoints: /actuator/health — app zinda hai ya nahi
/actuator/info — app ki info
/actuator/metrics — performance metrics
/actuator/env — environment properties
Production mein sensitive endpoints secure karo!

concepts.name: Custom Health Indicator
concepts.explanation: Apna custom health check banao — database, external service, disk space jo bhi check karna ho.
keyPoints: HealthIndicator implement karo custom health checks ke liye
Health.up() ya Health.down() return karo
withDetail() se extra info daalo
MeterRegistry se custom metrics banao
Prometheus + Grafana se monitoring dashboards banao

concepts.name: Profiles
concepts.explanation: Profiles se alag alag environments (dev, test, prod) ke liye alag settings rakho. Development mein H2 database, production mein MySQL — same code, alag config.
concepts.code:
# application.properties (common)
spring.application.name=my-app

# application-dev.properties
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:h2:mem:devdb
spring.jpa.show-sql=true
logging.level.root=DEBUG

# application-prod.properties
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://prod-server:3306/proddb
spring.jpa.show-sql=false
logging.level.root=WARN

# Active profile set karo
# Option 1: properties mein
spring.profiles.active=dev

# Option 2: command line se
# java -jar app.jar --spring.profiles.active=prod

# Option 3: environment variable se
# SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod
keyPoints: application-{profile}.properties profile-specific settings ke liye
spring.profiles.active se active profile set karo
Common settings base application.properties mein rakho
Command line ya env variable se override kar sakte ho
Multiple profiles active ho sakte hain: spring.profiles.active=dev,cloud

concepts.name: Docker Deployment
concepts.explanation: Docker se app ko container mein pack karo — 'mere machine pe chalta hai' wali problem khatam! Har jagah same environment milega.
concepts.code:
# Dockerfile
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

# Multi-stage build (smaller image)
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN ./mvnw package -DskipTests

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      - SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod
      - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:mysql://db:3306/mydb
    depends_on:
      - db
  db:
    image: mysql:8
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: mydb
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"
keyPoints: Multi-stage build se image size chhoti hoti hai
JRE image use karo (JDK nahi chahiye production mein)
docker-compose se app + database ek saath chalaao
Environment variables se Spring properties override karo
Health check add karo Docker container mein

concepts.name: Flyway
concepts.explanation: Database schema versioning ke liye Flyway use karo. SQL files with version numbers likho — Flyway automatically execute karta hai order mein. Team mein sab ko same DB schema milega!
concepts.code:
# pom.xml mein add karo
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
    <artifactId>flyway-core</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.properties
spring.flyway.enabled=true
spring.flyway.locations=classpath:db/migration

# File naming convention: V{version}__{description}.sql
# resources/db/migration/V1__create_users_table.sql
CREATE TABLE users (
    id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

# V2__add_phone_to_users.sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20);

# V3__create_orders_table.sql  
CREATE TABLE orders (
    id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    user_id BIGINT REFERENCES users(id),
    total DECIMAL(10,2)
);
keyPoints: SQL files version number ke saath likho: V1__, V2__, V3__
Flyway automatically order mein execute karta hai
Executed migrations dobara run nahi hote
Team mein sab ka database consistent rehta hai
resources/db/migration/ mein files rakho

concepts.name: Liquibase
concepts.explanation: Flyway ka alternative — XML, YAML, JSON ya SQL mein migrations likho. Zyada flexible hai. Changelogs mein changes track hote hain.
concepts.code:
# pom.xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.liquibase</groupId>
    <artifactId>liquibase-core</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.properties
spring.liquibase.change-log=classpath:db/changelog/db.changelog-master.yaml

# db/changelog/db.changelog-master.yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  - changeSet:
      id: 1
      author: developer
      changes:
        - createTable:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: id
                  type: BIGINT
                  autoIncrement: true
                  constraints:
                    primaryKey: true
              - column:
                  name: name
                  type: VARCHAR(100)
                  constraints:
                    nullable: false
  - changeSet:
      id: 2
      author: developer
      changes:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: phone
                  type: VARCHAR(20)
keyPoints: XML, YAML, JSON ya SQL mein migrations
changeSet id aur author ke saath track hota hai
Rollback support Flyway se better
Zyada flexible but Flyway se complex bhi

concepts.name: Method Level Security
concepts.explanation: Sirf URL pe hi nahin, method level pe bhi security lagao! @PreAuthorize se role check karo method call hone se pehle. Fine-grained access control possible hota hai.
keyPoints: @EnableMethodSecurity enable karo pehle
@PreAuthorize — method call SE PEHLE check karo
@PostAuthorize — method RETURN ke baad check karo
SpEL expressions se complex conditions likhi ja sakti hain
#paramName se method parameters access karo

concepts.name: Password Encoding
concepts.explanation: Passwords kabhi plain text store mat karo! BCrypt use karo — strong hashing algorithm. PasswordEncoder Spring Security mein built-in hai.
keyPoints: BCryptPasswordEncoder recommended hai
.encode() raw password ko hash karta hai
.matches() login ke waqt compare karta hai
Same password har baar alag hash generate karta hai (salt)
Kabhi plain text passwords store mat karo!

concepts.name: OAuth2 Resource Server
concepts.explanation: Third-party login (Google, GitHub) ya JWT tokens verify karna — OAuth2 Resource Server se karo. application.properties mein issuer URI set karo aur Spring sab handle kar leta hai!
keyPoints: issuer-uri se JWT validation automatic hota hai
JwtAuthenticationConverter se custom claims map karo
Google/GitHub ke tokens verify kar sakte ho
Stateless authentication — server pe session nahi rakhna

concepts.name: MockMvc Advanced
concepts.explanation: MockMvc se controllers test karo bina actual server chalaye. JSON assertions, status checks, header verification — sab ek jagah. Integration testing ke liye perfect!
keyPoints: @WebMvcTest sirf web layer load karta hai — fast!
@MockBean se service layer mock karo
.andExpect() se response validate karo
jsonPath() se JSON response check karo
status() se HTTP status code validate karo

concepts.name: TestContainers
concepts.explanation: Real database ke saath test karo bina local installation ke! TestContainers Docker containers spin up karta hai test ke liye. H2 in-memory se zyada realistic tests milte hain.
keyPoints: @Testcontainers + @Container se Docker container start hota hai
@DynamicPropertySource se test properties inject karo
Real database ke saath tests — zyada reliable
Docker zaroor installed hona chahiye

concepts.name: Security Testing
concepts.explanation: Secured endpoints test karo @WithMockUser se. Admin access, user access, unauthorized access — sab test karo. Security properly kaam kar rahi hai ye confirm karo!
keyPoints: @WithMockUser se mock authenticated user banao
roles = {"ADMIN"} se specific role se test karo
status().isUnauthorized() — 401
status().isForbidden() — 403
Security properly configured hai ye test karna zaroori hai

concepts.name: Application Events
concepts.explanation: Components ke beech loose coupling maintain karo events se! Publisher event fire karta hai, multiple listeners sunते hain. Order order service ko email service ke baare mein jaanna zaroori nahi!
keyPoints: ApplicationEvent extend karo custom event ke liye
ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent() se fire karo
@EventListener se listen karo
Loose coupling — publisher ko listener ki zaroorat nahi
@Async ke saath background mein execute karo

concepts.name: @Async — Background Tasks
concepts.explanation: Time lene wale tasks background mein chalao — user wait mat kare! Email bhejna, report generate karna — sab async karo. @EnableAsync zaroor lagao!
keyPoints: @EnableAsync enable karo pehle
@Async — method alag thread mein chalta hai
CompletableFuture return karo result ke liye
void return karo fire-and-forget tasks ke liye
ThreadPoolTaskExecutor se thread pool customize karo

concepts.name: @Scheduled — Cron Jobs
concepts.explanation: Regular tasks automatically chalao — cleanup, reports, notifications. Cron expression ya fixedDelay/fixedRate use karo. @EnableScheduling zaroor lagao!
keyPoints: @EnableScheduling enable karo
fixedRate — pehle run se N ms baad
fixedDelay — pichle run ke end se N ms baad
cron — specific time schedule (6 fields: sec min hr day month weekday)
cron "0 0 2 * * *" = har din 2 AM pe

concepts.name: @Cacheable — Caching
concepts.explanation: Same data baar baar database se mat lo — cache karo! @Cacheable se result ek baar fetch hota hai, baaki baar cache se milta hai. Slow queries ke liye life changer!
keyPoints: @EnableCaching enable karo
@Cacheable — result cache karo
@CacheEvict — cache mein se hatao
@CachePut — cache update karo (DB bhi call hoga)
By default ConcurrentHashMap use hota hai, Redis/Caffeine bhi lag sakta hai

concepts.name: Logging Configuration
concepts.explanation: Production mein logs hi teri aankhein hain! SLF4J + Logback default hai Spring Boot mein. Log levels, file output, format — sab configure karo.
keyPoints: SLF4J + Logback Spring Boot mein default hai
Log levels: TRACE < DEBUG < INFO < WARN < ERROR
Production mein INFO ya WARN use karo — zyada logs slow kar dete hain
@Slf4j (Lombok) se Logger manually banane ki zaroorat nahi
Parameterized logging use karo: log.info("User: {}", userId)

concepts.name: Project Structure
concepts.explanation: Spring Boot project ko organized rakho — har layer ka apna kaam hai. Controller → Service → Repository. Clean architecture se code maintainable rehta hai.
keyPoints: Controller: sirf HTTP request/response handle kare, logic nahi
Service: business logic yahan likho
Repository: database operations yahan
DTO: request/response ke liye alag classes — entity directly expose mat karo
Config: configurations alag package mein rakho

concepts.name: Pagination & Sorting
concepts.explanation: Bade data ko page by page serve karo — 10 lakh records ek baar mat bhejo! Spring Data pagination built-in support deta hai.
keyPoints: Pageable use karo — page number, size, sort direction
Page<T> total count, pages, aur content return karta hai
Default values set karo page aur size ke liye
Multiple sort fields support hote hain
Performance: pagination database level pe hoti hai (LIMIT/OFFSET)

concepts.name: Caching
concepts.explanation: Baar baar same data database se laane ki jagah cache mein rakh lo. Pehli baar database se aayega, baad mein cache se — bahut fast!
keyPoints: @EnableCaching application class pe lagao
@Cacheable result cache karta hai — same params = cache se aayega
@CachePut cache update karta hai (method hamesha chalta hai)
@CacheEvict cache se entry hatata hai
Redis ya EhCache production mein use karo, default in-memory hai

concepts.name: Scheduling & Async
concepts.explanation: Scheduled tasks aur async operations — email bhejno background mein, reports raat ko generate karo, etc.
keyPoints: @EnableScheduling main class pe lagao
fixedRate = milliseconds mein interval
cron expression se complex schedules banao
@Async method alag thread mein chalta hai
@EnableAsync main class pe lagao async ke liye
